ddls:
CREATE TABLE "python_releases" (
  "release_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each Python release */,
  "version_id" INTEGER /* ID of the version */,
  "architecture_id" INTEGER /* ID of the supported architecture */,
  "url" TEXT /* URL for downloading the Python release */,
  "release_date" TEXT /* Date when the release was officially released */,
  "release_notes" TEXT /* Detailed release notes or changelog */,
  "is_active" INTEGER /* Boolean flag to indicate if the release is currently active */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the release (e.g., beta, stable, deprecated) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("release_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_python_releases_version_id FOREIGN KEY ("version_id") REFERENCES versions ("version_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_python_releases_architecture_id FOREIGN KEY ("architecture_id") REFERENCES architectures ("architecture_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "architectures" (
  "architecture_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each architecture */,
  "architecture_name" TEXT /* Name of the system architecture */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the architecture */,
  "os_family" TEXT /* Operating system family (e.g., Windows, Linux, macOS) */,
  "processor_type" TEXT /* Processor type (e.g., x86, ARM) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("architecture_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "versions" (
  "version_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each version */,
  "version_prefix" TEXT /* Major and minor version number */,
  "version_suffix" TEXT /* Beta or other suffix indicating the release status */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the version */,
  "release_date" TEXT /* Date when the version was first released */,
  "is_latest" INTEGER /* Boolean flag to indicate if the version is the latest release in its series */,
  "is_beta" INTEGER /* Boolean flag to indicate if the version is a beta release */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("version_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "release_links" (
  "link_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each download link */,
  "release_id" INTEGER /* ID of the Python release */,
  "url" TEXT /* URL for downloading the Python release */,
  "file_size" REAL /* Size of the release file in MB */,
  "upload_date" TEXT /* Date the file was uploaded */,
  "checksum" TEXT /* Checksum of the file for integrity verification */,
  "download_count" INTEGER /* Number of times the file has been downloaded */,
  "is_direct_link" INTEGER /* Boolean flag to indicate if the link is a direct download link */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("link_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_release_links_release_id FOREIGN KEY ("release_id") REFERENCES python_releases ("release_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "projects" (
  "project_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each project */,
  "project_name" TEXT /* Name of the project */,
  "release_id" INTEGER /* ID of the Python release used by the project */,
  "deployment_date" TEXT /* Date when the Python release was deployed for the project */,
  "project_status" TEXT /* Current status of the project (e.g., development, testing, production) */,
  "last_updated" TEXT /* Last date the project details were updated */,
  "environment" TEXT /* Environment type (e.g., dev, test, prod) */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who last updated the project */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("project_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_projects_release_id FOREIGN KEY ("release_id") REFERENCES python_releases ("release_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_projects_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., developer, admin) */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Last login date of the user */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Date the user account was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Last date the user account was updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)
